title: Halving the cost of quantum addition
abstract: Improves the T-gate cost of an n-bit adder from 8n+O(1) to 4n+O(1) via a 'temporary logical-AND' construction: 4 T gates to store the AND of two qubits into an ancilla, and 0 T gates (measurement-based) to later uncompute/erase it. Equivalent to Jones' construction but framed as broadly applicable to integer/modular arithmetic, rotation synthesis, QFT, Shor, and Grover oracles.
relevance_note: TEMPORARY AND (logical-AND ancilla): compute-AND costs 4 T (1 Toffoli), uncompute via X-basis measurement + classically-controlled fixup costs 0 T. Halves the Toffoli/T cost of every adder and comparator in the EC point-add (modular add/sub, multiply). Foundational primitive that all later EC circuits build on.

title: A Classical-Quantum Adder with Constant Workspace and Linear Gates
abstract: Resolves the open question of whether classical-quantum adders (adding a classically-known constant into a quantum register) can match the O(n)-gate, O(1)-ancilla complexity of Cuccaro's quantum-quantum adder. Gives an adder using 3 clean ancillae and 4n±O(1) Toffolis, and a variant with 3n±O(1) Toffolis using 2 clean + n-2 dirty ancillae. Controlled versions require no extra workspace or Toffolis.
relevance_note: CONSTANT-ADDITION: modular-arithmetic circuits constantly add the classically-known modulus/curve constants; this gives a 3n-4n Toffoli, O(1)-clean-ancilla in-place constant adder, and crucially a *free* control (no extra Toffolis/space when conditioning on a control qubit). Directly cuts Toffoli + qubit cost of the modular reductions inside secp256k1 point-add.

title: Factoring with n+2 clean qubits and n-1 dirty qubits
abstract: Reversible classical circuits for arithmetic aided by dirty ancillae (extra qubits in an unknown state that must be restored). Reduces the clean-qubit count for n-bit Shor factoring from 1.5n+O(1) to n+2, assisted by n-1 dirty qubits, without increasing asymptotic size or depth.
relevance_note: DIRTY-ANCILLA ARITHMETIC: shows adders/incrementers that borrow qubits in an unknown state and restore them, slashing clean-qubit allocation. The qubit-count lever for any arithmetic-heavy register: reuse data registers as scratch instead of allocating fresh ancillae.

title: Windowed quantum arithmetic
abstract: A quantum analogue of classical windowing: small table lookups (QROM) let control qubits be processed in groups of w instead of one at a time. Presents windowed modular exponentiation with nested windowed modular multiplications, achieving lower Toffoli counts than prior work from tens to thousands of qubits.
relevance_note: WINDOWED ARITHMETIC: replace w per-bit controlled multiply-adds with a single QROM table lookup keyed on a w-bit window, then one addition. Trades 2^w-ish lookup Toffolis for a factor-~w reduction in multiply-add Toffolis. The dominant Toffoli-reduction lever for the scalar-multiplication / modular-multiply core of EC point-addition; pick w to balance lookup vs add cost.

title: Asymptotically Efficient Quantum Karatsuba Multiplication
abstract: Improves the space complexity of Karatsuba multiplication on a quantum computer from O(n^1.427) to O(n) while keeping O(n^lg3) gate complexity, by ensuring recursive calls add their outputs directly into subsections of the output register — avoiding storing and uncomputing intermediate results (a quantum analogue of tail-call optimization).
relevance_note: SPACE-EFFICIENT MULTIPLY: Karatsuba with O(n) ancilla instead of O(n^1.43) by writing recursive results in-place into output sub-registers (no intermediate store/uncompute). Qubit-count lever for the big-integer multiplies inside modular multiplication; technique generalizes to many recursive reversible algorithms.

title: Rise of conditionally clean ancillae for efficient quantum circuit constructions
abstract: Introduces 'conditionally clean' ancillae — qubits borrowed from existing system qubits (like dirty ancillae, no allocation) but treatable as initialized in a known state within a sub-computation (like clean ancillae, no toggle-detection overhead). Gives constructions including an n-controlled-NOT with 2n Toffolis in O(log n) depth using 2 clean ancillae, and an n-qubit incrementer with ~3n Toffolis, lowering gate counts/depth especially under tight ancilla budgets.
relevance_note: CONDITIONALLY-CLEAN ANCILLAE: borrow dirty system qubits but exploit a control condition to treat them as |0> within a subroutine — zero qubit allocation AND no dirty-ancilla toggle-detection penalty. Yields cheaper multi-controlled-NOT, incrementers, and comparators. Direct qubit+Toffoli lever for an ancilla-starved secp256k1 point-add where every borrowed qubit avoids a width increase.

title: A slightly smaller surface code S gate
abstract: A more space-efficient lattice-surgery construction for the logical S gate in the surface code.
relevance_note: Minor primitive: cheaper Clifford S in surface code; marginally reduces spacetime overhead of Clifford-heavy stretches. Low relevance to Toffoli/qubit count of the arithmetic itself.

title: How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits
abstract: End-to-end resource estimate combining Shor 1994, Griffiths-Niu semiclassical QFT, Zalka coset-representation/windowing, Fowler surface code, Ekerå-Håstad, and Gidney's temporary-AND + windowed arithmetic + AutoCCZ. Factors 2048-bit RSA in 8 hours with 20M physical qubits at 1e-3 error, 1µs cycle, 10µs reaction time, accounting for noise, routing, and distillation.
relevance_note: REFERENCE ESTIMATE & assembly: shows how to compose temporary-AND adders, windowed modular exponentiation, coset representation of modular integers, and semiclassical/approximate QFT into a full Shor pipeline with realistic surface-code costing. Template for assembling EC primitives into a costed end-to-end circuit.

title: How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers with less than a million noisy qubits
abstract: Cuts the 2019 Gidney-Ekerå estimate from 20M to <1M noisy qubits (factoring in <1 week, same physical assumptions). Qubit reduction comes mainly from approximate residue arithmetic (Chevignard-Fouque-Schrottenloher) replacing exact modular arithmetic, plus yoked surface codes and magic state cultivation for the fault-tolerant layer.
relevance_note: APPROXIMATE RESIDUE ARITHMETIC: tolerate small additive errors in modular arithmetic to drop register width and Toffoli count dramatically; combined with yoked codes + cultivation for ~20x physical-qubit reduction. Shows the approximate-arithmetic and FT-layer levers that also apply to ECC; pairs with the secp256k1 whitepaper.

title: Securing Elliptic Curve Cryptocurrencies against Quantum Vulnerabilities: Resource Estimates and Mitigations
abstract: New resource estimates for breaking the 256-bit Elliptic Curve Discrete Log Problem (the core of blockchain crypto). Shor's algorithm runs with either <1200 logical qubits and <90M Toffoli gates, or <1450 logical qubits and <70M Toffoli gates (a qubit/Toffoli tradeoff). On 1e-3 superconducting hardware with planar connectivity, executes in minutes using <500k physical qubits. Uses a zero-knowledge proof to validate the numbers without disclosing attack vectors, plus blockchain mitigation strategies.
relevance_note: DIRECTLY ON-TARGET: the current SOTA logical-qubit/Toffoli estimate for 256-bit ECDLP (secp256k1-class). Establishes the <1200q/<90M-Toffoli and <1450q/<70M-Toffoli frontier and the explicit qubit↔Toffoli tradeoff curve the point-add circuit is optimized against. The benchmark to beat.

title: Verifiable Quantum Advantage via Optimized DQI Circuits
abstract: Applies Decoded Quantum Interferometry to the Optimal Polynomial Intersection problem (dual code = Reed-Solomon), giving the first candidate for verifiable quantum advantage with optimal asymptotic speedup: classical hardness O(2^N) vs only Õ(N) quantum gates. The bottleneck reversible Reed-Solomon decoder needs an efficient Extended Euclidean Algorithm; introduces a new 'Dialog' representation for implicit Bézout-coefficient access and optimized in-place architectures that cut the leading-order cost.
relevance_note: DIALOG / EEA-SPLIT: the 'Dialog' implicit-Bezout representation and in-place Extended-Euclidean-Algorithm circuit. Modular inversion in affine EC point-addition is exactly an EEA/GCD computation — these in-place EEA constructions and the Dialog trick (avoid materializing both Bezout coefficients) are a direct Toffoli+qubit lever for the field-inversion step of secp256k1 affine point-add.

title: Efficient magic state factories with a catalyzed |CCZ> to 2|T> transformation
abstract: Magic-state factory constructions producing |CCZ> and |T> states. The |CCZ> factory applies lattice-surgery to the fault-tolerant Toffoli (footprint 12d×6d, one |CCZ> per 5.5d cycles). A catalyst |T> state then exactly transforms one |CCZ> into two |T> states, giving a |T| factory 25% smaller and twice as fast as Fowler et al.
relevance_note: DISTILLATION: |CCZ>→2|T> catalysis makes Toffoli/CCZ the natural resource unit and halves effective T-factory footprint. Since EC point-add cost is dominated by Toffoli/CCZ count, this fixes the spacetime price per Toffoli and motivates counting in Toffolis rather than T.

title: Low overhead quantum computation using lattice surgery
abstract: Shows lattice surgery reduces surface-code storage overhead by >4x and distillation overhead by ~5x versus defects/braids, enabling algorithms with 1e8 T gates using only 3.7e5 physical qubits at p~1e-3. Argues defects/braids should be deprecated in favor of lattice surgery.
relevance_note: LAYOUT: lattice surgery as the routing/storage substrate; sets the physical-qubit-per-logical-qubit and per-Toffoli spacetime budget that turns a logical Toffoli/qubit count into a physical-qubit estimate. Background for converting EC point-add logical costs to physical resources.

title: Flexible layout of surface code computations using AutoCCZ states
abstract: Constructs a self-correcting 'AutoCCZ' state with embedded delayed-choice CZs for completing gate teleportations, enabling efficient surface-code spacetime layouts for both depth-limited (ripple-carry addition) and Clifford-limited (QROM read) circuits. Finds that circuit-level Toffoli parallelism (e.g. carry-lookahead vs ripple-carry) does not reduce runtime below ~5M physical qubits.
relevance_note: AutoCCZ + QROM layout: self-correcting CCZ teleportation and efficient spacetime layout for QROM reads — the lookup tables that windowed arithmetic depends on. Confirms ripple-carry (small, serial) beats carry-lookahead (wide, parallel) at realistic qubit budgets, guiding the adder choice in a width-constrained point-add.

title: Magic state cultivation: growing T states as cheap as CNOT gates
abstract: Refines Knill/Jones/Chamberland/Bombin ideas to prepare |T> states by gradually 'cultivating' one state inside a single surface-code patch, using roughly as many physical gates as a lattice-surgery CNOT of equivalent reliability — an order of magnitude fewer qubit-rounds than prior distillation.
relevance_note: CULTIVATION: drops the per-T-state FT cost to ~CNOT level inside one patch, removing dedicated distillation factories. Lowers the physical-qubit multiplier on every Toffoli/T in the point-add; key to the <500k physical-qubit ECC figure.

title: Yoked surface codes
abstract: Nearly triples logical qubits per physical qubit in the teraquop regime by concatenating surface codes into high-density parity-check codes ('yokes' measured along rows, optionally columns, via lattice surgery), assuming only nearest-neighbor square-grid connectivity at p=1e-3.
relevance_note: YOKED CODES: ~3x more logical qubits per physical qubit for idle/storage qubits. Reduces the physical-qubit cost of the wide registers in an EC point-add; one of the FT-layer levers behind the recent qubit-count reductions.

title: Stim: a fast stabilizer circuit simulator
abstract: Stim, a fast stabilizer-circuit simulator using an improved tableau representation: analyzes a distance-100 surface code (20k qubits, 8M gates, 1M measurements) in 15s and samples full shots at 1 kHz, improving deterministic-measurement complexity from quadratic to linear.
relevance_note: TOOLING: the standard simulator for validating FT/QEC constructions and Clifford parts of circuits. Not an arithmetic optimization itself, but the verification backbone for any surface-code costing of a point-add pipeline.

title: New circuits and an open source decoder for the color code
abstract: Improved stabilizer-measurement circuits and an open-source decoder for the color code, which supports more efficient logical operations than the surface code at the cost of harder syndrome extraction/decoding.
relevance_note: Alternative FT substrate (color code) with cheaper logical gates; potential future lever for reducing the spacetime cost of Clifford-heavy or transversal portions of an arithmetic circuit. Lower direct relevance to Toffoli/qubit count.
